code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete functional failure despite proper usage attempts
    - Multi-purchase defects overriding any initial satisfaction
    - Usability failures preventing basic functionality
    - Explicit safety hazards with evidence of danger

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Technical adequacy with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Brief positive statements lacking enthusiasm ("works fine")
    - Usage limitations affecting functionality ("don't overstuff")
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full performance
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise ("Perfect!", "Love it!") without negatives
    - 3+ praised aspects (functionality + design + durability)
    - Close brand comparisons with satisfaction despite minor differences
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" endorsement despite imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works perfectly" →5 vs "Works fine" →4 (enthusiasm distinction)
    - Functional praise + usage limitation →4
    - Praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Not as good as X but still satisfied" →5 if needs met
    - Core function achieved despite setup →5 if flawless post-setup
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example1:
Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
<Reasoning>Brief factual statement without enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example2:
Review: "Stasher bag perfect for snacks! No more stale crackers."
<Reasoning>Concise enthusiastic praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example3:
Review: "This machine foils every cleaning effort - parts never function properly."
<Reasoning>Complete functional failure despite usage attempts →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example4:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences."
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example5:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns for heavy loads."
<Reasoning>Adequate performance with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example6:
Review: "Rollers handle bumps perfectly! Installation took muscle."
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellence outweighs setup effort →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example7:
Review: "Does the job but constantly needs readjusting. Exhausting."
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds needed →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example8:
Review: "Vibrant colors need ironing but worth it for durability."
<Reasoning>Non-functional maintenance vs core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example9:
Review: "We own lots of Lodge. This isn't better but comes close. Daily driver now."
<Reasoning>Satisfaction despite minor inferiority to top brand →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example10:
Review: "Second unit failed faster than first. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (31 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 2 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This mattress get 4 stars for comfort, but there's so many other nice features about it that it gets 5 starts for everything else. To begin with, I wouldn't suggest using this as a long term every day bed for someone. The shallow design and budget pr..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This bathroom rug is so plush and soft. One of my dogs tried to claim it while I had it laid out on the couch. It is 100% organic cotton that is sustainable. So you help the environment and the natural resources. This rug is machine washable, gentle ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
